risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Using a computer-based randomization plan generator, the 14 schools will be randomized to the control or intervention condition from within each pair following baseline assessments." Comment: This describes a computer-based randomization procedure within matched pairs, which indicates a genuinely random allocation sequence was used.

Quote: "Using a computer-based randomization plan generator, the 14 schools will be randomized to the control or intervention condition from within each pair following baseline assessments. A researcher not associated with recruitment or data collection, and who will be blind to school identity, will carry out randomization procedures." Comment: The allocation was performed after baseline by a researcher independent of recruitment/data collection and blind to school identity, which supports concealment from the recruiters and prevents foreknowledge of upcoming assignment. Although the paper does not explicitly say 'central allocation' or 'sealed envelopes,' the described procedure is a secure allocation mechanism that prevents the person enrolling schools from knowing the next assignment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Teachers will be aware of their allocation to the intervention or control condition." Comment: Participants delivering the intervention (teachers) were not blinded. The intervention is explicitly behavior-changing and delivered by teachers in PE lessons, so knowledge of allocation could plausibly influence the primary and secondary outcomes (e.g., MVPA during PE lessons, sedentary time, student motivation and engagement), making performance bias a dominant concern across study outcomes.

Quote: "Trained research assistants who will be blinded to school allocation will conduct baseline, post-intervention and maintenance phase assessments." Comment: Outcome assessment was performed by trained research assistants who were explicitly blinded to allocation. The key primary outcome (MVPA during PE lessons) was measured by accelerometers with standardized processing, and other objective outcomes were likewise collected using devices or records, so knowledge of allocation would be unlikely to influence measurement.

Quote: "The trial will ... use generalized mixed models, enabling the inclusion of all participants (included at baseline) in the analyses." Comment: The protocol explicitly states that all baseline participants will be included in intention-to-treat analyses using generalized mixed models, and it anticipates up to 30% loss to follow-up without substantial impact on power. Although exact attrition numbers are not yet reported in this protocol, the planned analysis approach and the absence of any signal of differential or outcome-related dropout make attrition bias unlikely.
